notes: Pi reset (`/new`) supersession. Pi's Source is FilesUnderRoot(~/.pi/agent/sessions): a transient proc-<PID> pre-session is born ready at launch, hands off to a rollout-keyed UUID-1 session on the first prompt, runs one turn, then the reset (driven via the reset_session step → pi's in-REPL `/new`: interactive-mode.js:2037 → handleClearCommand → runtimeHost.newSession → session-manager.js:484 newSession() mints a fresh createSessionId() and a new <ts>_<uuid2>.jsonl path, same PID, file flushed lazily on the first post-reset message) + a second prompt materializes a SECOND rollout (UUID-2, parentSession:null, no UUID-1 history). The reset's user-observable signal is v2_session_birth (new_session:true) — a session_id never seen before appears in ready; the new session superseding the old IS the expected behavior. The driver's reset_session step + multi-session session.uuids capture BOTH rollouts, so the curated fixture contains UUID-1 and UUID-2 and these phases validate against a committed recording. As with codex, no transcript_removed fires at the `/new` keystroke ITSELF (reset is lazy — UUID-2 appears on the first post-reset prompt; the old UUID-1 FILE then lingers frozen on disk). The removal IS produced — both rollouts share one PID, so when UUID-2's PID is discovered the daemon's same-PID cleanup (#169, the SAME path as claudecode/codex) emits transcript_removed for UUID-1 ~ms after the new pid_discovered (NOT a readyTTL idle sweep). There is deliberately NO old_session_ended phase: a fixture scoping choice (v2_session_birth is the canonical reset proxy), not because pi never produces a removal.

text: Pre-session row (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 1s of pi launch
After the first prompt lands, the rollout-keyed session appears in ready — proc-<PID> hands off to a fresh UUID
First user prompt drives ready → working on the rollout session
First turn completes back to ready (same rollout UUID)

Spec: 'New session appears in ready with a NEW session_id' — after /new, the first post-reset prompt spawns a fresh rollout UUID never seen before; this is the strongest observable proxy for 'the conversation was reset'
Second user prompt drives the NEW session ready → working (must NOT match a stale working transition on UUID-1)
Second turn completes back to ready on the new session
